summary: react-simple-utils-kit exfiltrates Coze API tokens and tenant data on install
details: Package is advertised as 'a simple date formatting utility for React projects' but ships a postinstall.js that performs credential and data exfiltration on npm install. The script reads COZE_WORKLOAD_API_TOKEN, COZE_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_CLIENT_ID, COZE_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_CLIENT_SECRET, COZE_WORKSPACE_ID, COZE_PROJECT_ID and related env vars, then uses the installer's bearer token to enumerate Coze tenant resources via api.coze.cn (workspaces, bots, workflows, knowledges, conversations, api_keys, plugins). Each response is POSTed to a hardcoded plain-HTTP attacker collector at http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com (a public out-of-band pentest collector domain). It additionally exchanges the workload-identity client_id/client_secret for an access_token at api.coze.cn/.well-known/token and ships the full token response — including client_secret reflection and the issued access_token — to the same collector, granting the attacker long-lived access to the installer's Coze tenant. The mismatch between the advertised date-utility purpose (index.js only contains formatDate helpers) and the postinstall harvester confirms deceptive packaging.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
